style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#FFFFFF;strokeColor=#2563EB;strokeWidth=2;align=left;spacingLeft=12;verticalAlign=middle;fontFamily=Inter;fontColor=#0F172A;" value="Default rule
- Authoriser, Tenant API, BFF, Billing, Gateway interceptors stay outside the VPC
- Public AWS regional endpoints do not justify VPC attachment by themselves" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="150" width="1250" x="100" y="560" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="exception_lane" parent="1" style="swimlane;startSize=34;rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#FEF3C7;strokeColor=#D97706;strokeWidth=2;fontStyle=1;fontFamily=Inter;fontColor=#0F172A;" value="Exception Path: Private Dependency Forces VPC Attachment" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="860" width="1390" x="1530" y="180" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="adapter" parent="exception_lane" style="sketch=0;outlineConnect=0;fontColor=#1f2937;gradientColor=#F5B342;gradientDirection=north;fillColor=#D86613;strokeColor=#ffffff;dashed=0;verticalLabelPosition=bottom;verticalAlign=top;align=center;html=1;fontSize=11;aspect=fixed;shape=mxgraph.aws4.resourceIcon;resIcon=mxgraph.aws4.lambda;fontFamily=Inter;fontColor=#0F172A;" value="Narrow VPC Adapter / Worker" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="90" width="90" x="130" y="210" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="vpclane_note" parent="exception_lane" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#FFFFFF;strokeColor=#D97706;strokeWidth=2;align=left;spacingLeft=12;verticalAlign=middle;fontFamily=Inter;fontColor=#0F172A;" value="Exception rule
- Keep the blast radius local
- Put only the private-facing adapter in isolated subnets
- Do not drag the shared northbound control plane into VPC networking" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="110" width="1180" x="90" y="60" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="endpoints" parent="exception_lane" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#F8FAFC;strokeColor=#CBD5E1;strokeWidth=2;align=left;spacingLeft=12;verticalAlign=middle;fontFamily=Inter;fontColor=#0F172A;" value="Required VPC contracts
- Subnets
- Security groups
- VPC endpoints
- Route tables
- DNS" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="150" width="290" x="360" y="180" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="private_dep" parent="exception_lane" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#D1FAE5;strokeColor=#059669;strokeWidth=2;fontStyle=1;align=left;spacingLeft=12;verticalAlign=middle;fontFamily=Inter;fontColor=#0F172A;" value="Private dependency
RDS / ElastiCache / Private ALB / PrivateLink / Tenant private network" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="150" width="440" x="810" y="180" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="exception_doc" parent="exception_lane" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#FFFFFF;strokeColor=#059669;strokeWidth=2;align=left;spacingLeft=12;verticalAlign=middle;fontFamily=Inter;fontColor=#0F172A;" value="Each VPC exception must document
- exact private dependency
- why public endpoints are insufficient
- endpoint and SG inventory
- rollback path to non-VPC if the dependency disappears" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="170" width="1080" x="170" y="470" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="decision" parent="1" style="rhombus;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#FFF7ED;strokeColor=#D97706;strokeWidth=2;fontStyle=1;fontFamily=Inter;fontColor=#0F172A;" value="Private-only dependency?" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="130" width="220" x="1360" y="480" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="legend" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#FFFFFF;strokeColor=#334155;strokeWidth=2;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;fontFamily=Inter;fontColor=#0F172A;" value="Legend: Blue edges = request/control-plane access. Green edges = private dependency path. Purple dashed edges = isolate behind adapter. Amber dashed edges = decision / exception boundary." vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="70" width="2870" x="50" y="1080" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="e1" edge="1" parent="1" source="api" div.div:
Default rule
- Authoriser, Tenant API, BFF, Billing, Gateway interceptors stay outside the VPC
- Public AWS regional endpoints do not justify VPC attachment by themselves

div.div:
Exception rule
- Keep the blast radius local
- Put only the private-facing adapter in isolated subnets
- Do not drag the shared northbound control plane into VPC networking

display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Inter; color: light-dark(#0F172A, #d3daea); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
Required VPC contracts
- Subnets
- Security groups
- VPC endpoints
- Route tables
- DNS

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(209, 250, 229), rgb(4, 39, 21)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(5, 150, 105), rgb(42, 167, 128));

div.div:
Private dependency
RDS / ElastiCache / Private ALB / PrivateLink / Tenant private network

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(5, 150, 105), rgb(42, 167, 128));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1066px; height: 1px; padding-top: 716px; margin-left: 1685px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #0F172A;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Inter; color: light-dark(#0F172A, #d3daea); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
Each VPC exception must document
- exact private dependency
- why public endpoints are insufficient
- endpoint and SG inventory
- rollback path to non-VPC if the dependency disappears

div.div: Legend: Blue edges = request/control-plane access. Green edges = private dependency path. Purple dashed edges = isolate behind adapter. Amber dashed edges = decision / exception boundary.
